items.title: chore(release): prepare font packages 5.3.0
items.body: This updates the base CLI to create new packages with v5.3 and updates the relevant changelog.

items.title: refactor(api): use hono node server adapter
fix(fs): preserve unicode path spelling
items.body:
Related: https://github.com/withastro/astro/issues/17381

This PR preserves archive and destination Unicode spelling instead of rewriting names to NFD. We inherited this unicode cache from `node-tar`, but since we don't rely on their concurrent path reservation system, we actually didn't need to copy over this unicode mitigation for every path.

items.title: ci(release): capitalize workflow name
items.body: Hopefully resolves release flows not triggering.

items.title: ci(release): use GitHub App client ID
items.body: Updates the release workflow to use the current GitHub App client ID input.\n\nSetup required before merge: add the RELEASE_APP_CLIENT_ID Actions variable and RELEASE_APP_PRIVATE_KEY Actions secret.

items.title: feat(registry): add ingestion tooling
items.body:
Related: #39, #773, #588

Adds deterministic, text-only Google Fonts and NAM ingestion from exact Git commits. The committed registry owns normalized source metadata and explicitly reviewed package policy; generation validates existing policy but never derives it from legacy catalogs or changes it.

The tooling runs as a private pnpm workspace on Node 24, rejects shallow repositories, inspects source fonts through Core, preserves removed families, and validates canonical output. It does not change API, R2, npm, or package behavior.

Validation:
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm --filter fontsource-registry-tools typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter fontsource-registry-tools test`
- full persisted-registry validation

items.title: fix(publish): stabilize changed package order
items.body: Related: https://github.com/fontsource/fontsource/actions/runs/29648886818/job/88091695122

items.title: chore(repo): modernize monorepo tooling
items.body: Migrates repository to pnpm catalogs and Node. Also cleans up our CI and tooling monorepo wide.

items.title: feat(core): add source font inspection
items.body: Adds some inspection tools in the core package using Glypht in preparation for some analysis tooling.

items.title: fix(cli): generate variable index css fallback
items.body: Closes #1024.

items.title: fix(cli): add css type declarations
items.body: Closes #1086.

items.title: feat(api): add aggregate stats badges
items.body:
Related: #1170

Adds Shields-compatible endpoints for aggregate npm and jsDelivr monthly and total download counts. The responses reuse the existing D1-backed stats path and its 24-hour Worker cache policy.

Updates the root README badges to use the Fontsource API, documents the endpoints, and regenerates the website API client. This removes the remaining dependency on `download-stat-aggregator` from this repository.

items.title: feat(api): serve stats from D1
items.body:
Related: #1169

Serve `/v1/stats` and `/v1/stats/:id` from the completed D1 dataset.

This also removes the old KV stats blob, scheduled aggregator refresh, and `download-stat-aggregator` URLs. The README badges are deliberately left for a follow-up after the cutover is live.

items.title: fix(api): throttle npm stats backfill
items.body: We're still getting 429'd by NPM more than I like while backfilling, so this makes things more lenient.

items.title: refactor(api): promote combined worker
items.body: Cleans up old legacy API code with the new API worker which has now been moved from `api/worker/` to `api/`.

items.title: fix(website): add open graph site name
items.body: Additional OG site name tweak.

items.title: fix(api): correct stats ingestion data
items.body: Tweaks some missing ingest data issues.

items.title: feat(website): pin cdn urls to package versions
items.body:
Closes #1042. This makes the default CDN copy paste blocks to use versioned URLs for long lived immutable caches as well as updating our documentation to avoid floating tags and naturally promote pinned URLs.

This ideally pushes better practices to rely on the immutable caches.

items.title: feat(website): add dynamic font social images
items.body: Closes #557.

items.title: fix(api): back off npm stats ingestion
items.body: Handle NPM API rate-limiting us more gracefully.

items.title: refactor(website): use generated api client
items.body: Migrates the website to use [HeyAPI](https://heyapi.dev/) which simplifies maintaining custom types for our API and auto-generates it all.

items.title: perf(website): optimize algolia search
items.body: Add a projection to Algolia search so we only need to fetch half of the properties we have stored, because we don't need all of it to render the `Hit` component.

items.title: feat(api): serve minified CSS aliases
items.body: Closes #1084. This adds `.min.css` alias to all our CDN endpoints.

items.title: refactor(website): simplify utilities
items.body: Removes some dependencies and uses the Algolia ESM imports now. Also uses React Wrap Balancer on some documentation headers which renders nicer on mobile.

items.title: fix(api): handle missing font packages
items.body: This PR handles missing or variable-only font packages without repeatedly starting failing artifact containers. Additionally, invalid pinned versions now return 404, while valid variable-only downloads continue to work.

items.title: fix(website): use public API domain for downloads
items.body: Switch back to the official domain now that we cleanly cutover to the new download API.

items.title: feat(api): ingest download stats internally
items.body:
This aims to replace [download-stat-aggregator](https://github.com/fontsource/download-stat-aggregator) with a D1-backed NPM stat aggregator via our API.

This mimics the logic outlined in this [Tanstack blog post](https://tanstack.com/blog/npm-stats-the-right-way) which does require us to run things more in the cloud and incrementally.

Currently this will _ONLY_ ingest data for the D1 database, but we won't serve it yet until it is fully populated.

items.title: fix(api): build artifacts from published package files
items.body: Remove manifest logic and instead rely on the NPM tarball contents which simplifies any issues if our build CLI and API differ in logic (which it did for `playwrite-us-modern`).

items.title: test(api): trigger deploy
items.body: Triggers a deploy in CI.

items.title: fix(api): destroy completed containers
items.body: Containers aren't properly cleaning up, so this forces a deletion code side to make it explicit.

items.title: fix(website): prevent variable axis control overlap
items.body: Tweak the variable font selector container to wrap better if the variable axis name is really long.

items.title: refactor(api): use one-shot package builders
items.body: This simplifies and cuts the builder container download path to simplify any branching and default to fully building a package  for every container request, which should also minimize our container usage in the long run.

items.title: chore(worker): raise container concurrency limit
items.body: Raises the container concurrency limit for building font packages to handle more bursty workloads.

items.title: feat(worker): optimize placement and container idling
items.body: Sets the artifact builder sleep to one second since once the job is done, we don't need to do anything else and not waste idle quota. Also enables smart placement.

items.title: fix(download): show archive ready state
items.body:
Related: #1142

Shows some user feedback on when the download worker is ready to pull from the bucket.

items.title: perf(api): speed up cold download builds
items.body:
This significantly speeds up cold cache zipped downloads on the new API worker by instead downloading the NPM tarballs and restitching them vs making 2k+ requests and stitching that instead. It is more CPU intensive, but significantly better than network latecy.

This makes the UX a lot more positive and relies on our NPM tarballs which are stable contracts to rely on.

items.title: fix(api): remove versioned support on zip downloads
items.body:
Hugely simplifies our download endpoint logic in the new API worker by removing versioning on the zipped endpoint (which is an undocumented endpoint so I'm fine with breaking it).

Static and variable font packages can be completely different in versioning e.g. 

```
/fonts/cormorant-garamond@5.2.11/download.zip
/fonts/cormorant-garamond:vf@5.2.6/download.zip
```

Because variable font support might have been added later by an upstream provider.

This makes versioning on the combined zip file to effectively be incorrect and I don't believe we need to have versioned support for this specific edge case. So let's remove it.

items.title: feat(api): return 202 while downloads build
items.body:
Related: #1142

Returns `202 Accepted` with `Retry-After` while a cold download archive is built by the container. A named Durable Object coordinates one in-flight build per resolved font version, subsequent polls serve the R2 archive, and builder failures remain visible as raw API errors.

items.title: perf(api): speed up cold download builds
items.body: This tweaks the download logic and parallelize what else we could to help cold cache updates. It's a minimal gain, but would be useful for smaller package.

items.title: feat(website): add favorites and collections
items.body:
This adds the concept of collections and favorites to the website, where people can keep track of what fonts they actually like and refer to it again.

The implementation is purely managed in local state and is managed by Legend State for the persistence model.

items.title: fix(download): migrate website to new worker
items.body:
Use the new download worker under the hood for the website.

This adds a new downloading page that gives a better UX feel, especially when we are creating a fresh bundle from no cache.

It also updates the API worker to properly return errors in a format that can be displayed correctly on the FE error boundary.

items.title: fix(api): tweak container builder instance class
items.body: Also enables the catalog cron job.

items.title: fix(api): validate downloads before container build
items.body: To prevent spinning up unnecessary containers, we should pre-validate everything in the worker layer first so only valid requests hit the container builders.

items.title: refactor(api): flatten download archive layout
items.body: This flattens the download API zip structure and removes the nested `ttf/` and `webfonts/` directories. It's now a much flatter directory with all the files in one place.

items.title: fix(website): serialize carbon ad refreshes
items.body: Tab switching really quickly can cause some of the Carbon placements to double render badly. This serializes them so we don't have this race condition occur.

items.title: fix(api): use jsdelivr flat structure query
items.body: Fixes invalid `jsdelivr` metadata URL.

items.title: perf(website): use api service binding
items.body:
This switches the website to use a service binding to the API directly vs making a subrequest to `api.fontsource.org`. Ideally making the website latencies even quicker as it can skip any DNS resolution or TCP etc.

It also then just runs as one worker invocation.

items.title: fix(website): prevent preview buttons disappearing on hover
items.body: Preserves the preview button foreground color on hover so the controls remain visible in both themes, verified in the browser and by the production website build.

items.title: fix(api): use new bucket for new worker
items.body: Removes staging environment and also uses a new R2 bucket for this worker.

items.title: fix(website): handle font loading failed state
items.body: This resolves a bug where the font loader hook spins infinitely causing the browser to hang if the font loader state fails. There is also another fix to normalize variable axis names which may all be uppercase.

items.title: perf(website): migrate to legend state v3
items.body: This migrates the website to use Legend State V3 and also makes some small performance tweaks and changes to better utilize Legend.

items.title: chore(wrangler): update toml naming
items.body: Tweak Wrangler TOML settings for the new API.

items.title: fix(website): add canonical SEO metadata and doc descriptions
items.body: Tweaks canonical SEO metadata to better align with Google requirements and added more document metadata.

items.title: perf(website): virtualize search results and smoother infinite scroll
items.body: This introduces React Virtuoso for proper virtualization of our infinite scroll, plus many tweaks to make infinite scroll feel smoother and faster.

items.title: docs(readme): remove stargazers
items.body: Closes #1123. I won't force a full package refresh with this change, but it should affect new packages down the line.

items.title: fix: preserve tar entry boundaries with PAX metadata
items.body: Fixes PAX size overrides that could desynchronize tar entry boundaries.

items.title: fix(fs): validate symlinks against destination state
items.body: Validates extracted symlinks against the destination's real filesystem state so pre-existing symlink components cannot redirect targets outside the extraction root.

items.title: fix(fs): prevent directory packing readdir race
items.body: Revalidates source directories after enumeration so swapped paths are skipped. Adds a regression test for the race.

items.title: fix(fs): prevent symlink parent races
items.body: Prevents symlink extraction from writing outside the destination when its parent changes during replacement.

items.title: fix: preserve equals signs in PAX values
items.body: Fixes a small bug where multiple `=` in a PAX header will end up truncated.

items.title: perf(website): use new cloudflare worker cache api
items.body: This uses the new [Cloudflare Workers Cache API](https://blog.cloudflare.com/workers-cache/) which should make the UX even more snappier (it already is ridiculously fast with global workers).

items.title: fix(docs): normalize trailing slash on html
items.body: Pre-rendering the docs causes some page loads to have a trailing slash on CF, so this enables a relevant setting and also makes the docs pages more resilient to this format.

items.title: fix(fs): reject symlink hardlink targets
items.body: Make hardlink validation even stricter rejecting symlink targets.

items.title: fix(website): align mobile font preview tags
items.body: Tweaks the font preview styles to behave nicer on mobile.

items.title: fix(pax): cap tar metadata entries
items.body: Adds an 8MB limit to tar metadata entries. It should ideally never be hit unless a malicious entry is trying to abuse it.

items.title: perf(web): speed up buffered unpack
items.body: This adds a fast-path on the web unpacker. If an input is already a buffer instead of a stream, we should directly unpack that instead of previously relying on converting it into a stream and reading that instead.

items.title: feat(website): refine font details links
items.body: Adds menus for the Github and NPM buttons on each font detail page, so you can differentiate between static and variable sources.

items.title: perf(website): prerender static routes
items.body: Pre-render some routes that are knowingly static such as documentation or tools.

items.title: fix(fs): harden unpack parent races
items.body:
Fixes unpack TOCTOU issues where paths were validated during header processing, but the actual file or hardlink operation happened later using the same string path. A concurrent mutation could replace a prepared parent directory with a symlink and redirect extraction outside the destination.

This opens regular file sinks before yielding for more body bytes, and revalidates deferred hardlink source/output parents at finalization before linking.

items.title: chore(website): migrate to react router v8
items.body: Migrates to React Router v8.

items.title: perf(website): lazy-load shiki highlighting
items.body: The code highlighter component is one of our biggest chunks, so splitting this up to load async and in smaller chunks makes this finally more manageable and optimal.

items.title: fix(fs): reject symlink graph follow overflow
items.body: Explicitly reject any symlink graphs that bypasses the expected size from the cache.

items.title: perf(website): cache only root algolia ssr state
items.body: This tweaks the Algolia SSR cache to be conditional. A lot of queries just write to the cache, but never actually call from it.

items.title: fix(fs): validate symlink graph after extraction
items.body:
Fixes a symlink chain traversal issue during filesystem extraction.

Previously, symlink targets were validated lexically when each entry was written. That caught direct escapes, but a later archive entry could create another symlink that changed how an earlier in-tree symlink resolved. This meant extraction could complete while leaving a symlink inside the destination that resolved outside the extraction root.

items.title: fix(fs): snapshot caller pack sources
items.body: Widens `packTar` array inputs to accept readonly arrays. Also snapshots sources so they can't mutate either.

items.title: fix(fs): harden pack against source swaps
items.body: This hardens `packTar` against mutable source races where a path can change after validation but before content is read.

items.title: feat(website): add boneyard skeletons
items.body: Experiments with adding [Boneyard](https://boneyard.vercel.app/overview) for skeleton loading.

items.title: perf(pax): use flag for header check
items.body: This uses a simple boolean check over an `Object.keys` check which would perform much better with larger pax tar files. Also trimes an unnecessary decoder.

items.title: perf(web): skip redundant buffer allocation
items.body: This skips a redundant array allocation especially since `streamToBuffer` already returns the expected return type.

items.title: feat(website): add carbon to docs
items.body: Add Carbon to the documentation and font converter tool page.

items.title: fix(algolia): clean up cache key logic for ssr
items.body:
This is a quick fix to reduce query param cache bombing from crawlers and agents that spam our Algolia KV records with many unnecessary writes.

This drops any unrecognized query params and also sorts them so we get better cache hits and not stress our KV so much.

items.title: fix(path): replace pre-existing link leaves without following them
items.body: Matches `node-tar` behaviour.

items.title: feat: add allow origins helper
items.body:
This adds a new `AllowOrigins` helper which is stricter than the component based `AllowHosts`.

While `AllowHosts` requires chaining with other components such as `AllowSchemes`, `AllowOrigins` fully matches a URL and can be even more explicit.

items.title: refactor(dashboard): migrate away from radix to mantine
items.body: We had a partial experiement migrating incrementally to Radix components vs Mantine, but it is not really worth it for the long term. This migrates all the Radix components back to Mantine.

items.title: chore(deps): upgrade all major fe
items.body: This is a major version upgrade for all FE dependencies (React, Vite, React Router, Mantine, Recharts, Typescript), which should clean things up bit nicely.

items.title: chore: upgrade deps and remove taskfile
items.body:
This does some cleaning up of the repository and fully migrates us away from `task` to just `mise`. Also adds relevant agent context in new `AGENTS.md` files and some general tooling.

Tweaks Renovate config to be less noisy as well.

items.title: fix: block mapped ipv6 ranges
items.body:
This PR blocks the IANA special-purpose IPv6 range `2620:4f:8000::/48` by default. That range was missing from specialDenyV6, so it could pass as a normal global IPv6 destination.

It blocks IPv4-mapped IPv6 literals like `https://[::ffff:8.8.8.8]` by default. Previously those were unwrapped too early and treated as ordinary IPv4, so mapped public IPv4 literals were allowed even though `::ffff:0:0/96` is a special-purpose IPv6 range.

items.title: feat(website): use fumadocs
items.body:
Migrate our website docs backend to use Fumadocs and generate LLMs.txt and other relevant modern goodies that documentation sites have.

This also gives us a nice refresh of our docs website which feels a lot closer in design with our homepage.

items.title: refactor(api): use single worker pattern
items.body:
This is a major rewrite of the Fontsource API to be entirely built on modern CF Workers and also start processing font package bundling/zipping using `@fontsource/core` Glypht functions and dropping our depending on Fly.io (love them still) to use Cloudflare Containers.

It also uses Chanfana for OpenAPI generation and it is finally architected in a way to be testable mostly relying on snapshots for simple high level coverage of our outputs.

Our old API design was quite brittle built on many chained workers via service bindings and consolidating all that would let us iterate more and maintain our API much more easily for the long run.

items.title: fix(website): use correct jsdelivr urls
items.body: Closes #1103.

items.title: refactor(core): cleanup and consolidate CSS API
items.body:
This cleans up the CSS API a bunch and preps the `core` package to be the underlying engine for the upcoming API rewrite. 

A couple bug fixes were included such as oblique style handling and also making the website more consistent with our package generation by deduplicating the logic in the website.

items.title: feat(website): add opensearch
items.body: Adds the OpenSearch spec.

items.title: fix(website): handle repeated query params
items.body: Repeated query params on subsets can cause a throw for the Algolia search handler. This cleans that up a little.

items.title: fix(website): use seconds for algolia ttl
items.body: The expiration TTL is in seconds, not milliseconds, so our Cloudflare cache was never expiring.

items.title: refactor(website): use new core package for conversion
items.body: Built on #1097. This relies on the new core package, runs Biome and also updates some of the core deps (Vite 8, React Router etc.)

items.title: feat(core): rework api and add high level conversion helpers
items.body: This tries to use more upstream Glypht helpers and adds high level conversion helper functions which should let us simplify code in our API and website significantly.

items.title: fix(fs): prevent file sink from following a symlink on unpack
items.body: Add the `NOFOLLOW` flag when opening a file to unpack to prevent an existing symlink from extracting outside the extraction root.
